course_outcomes.LO1: Analyze the time and space complexity of data structure operations using asymptotic notation.
course_outcomes.LO2: Implement fundamental data structures including stacks, queues, linked lists, trees, heaps, and hash tables.
course_outcomes.LO3: Select and justify the appropriate data structure for a given algorithmic problem.
course_outcomes.LO4: Reason about correctness of data structure implementations using invariants and formal arguments.
course_outcomes.LO5: Apply data structures to solve programming problems efficiently in a high-level language.
course_outcomes.LO6: Test and debug data structure implementations systematically.

program_outcomes.SO1: Analyze a complex computing problem and apply principles of computing and other relevant disciplines to identify solutions.
program_outcomes.SO2: Design, implement, and evaluate a computing-based solution to meet a given set of computing requirements.
program_outcomes.SO3: Communicate effectively in a variety of professional contexts.
program_outcomes.SO6: Apply computer science theory and software development fundamentals to produce computing-based solutions.
